compiled_state.title: Explore subagent used to search entire project in single dispatch — should parallelize into independent targeted queries
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
During the worktree isolation guard removal investigation, the orchestrator dispatched a single Explore subagent to search the entire project for both isolation guard code AND all references to it. These are independent searches that could have been parallelized as separate targeted Grep/Glob tool calls or two smaller subagent dispatches.

## Impact
Single large-scope subagents consume more context and run slower than parallel targeted queries. The pattern wastes time and risks context exhaustion for broad project-wide searches.

## Expected
Independent searches (e.g. 'find the hook file' vs 'find all auth marker references') should be issued as parallel Grep/Glob calls or parallel subagent dispatches, not bundled into a single exploratory subagent.

## Actual
Single Explore subagent dispatched with both goals, searching the entire project sequentially.

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed

comments.body: Behavioral assertion (LLM-behavioral exemption): Each Explore subagent dispatch MUST target a single, specific search objective. Current behavior: single Explore dispatched for multiple search objectives. Expected: explicit rule requiring separate parallel Explore dispatches per search objective.
comments.author: Test
